preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups needing an ai-first, intent-aware design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context: this is an AI-first, intent-aware design system built for startups. Movers need fast clarity on what this is and why it matters to them — no ambiguity, straight to the point.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. Movers respect proof but don't need lengthy narratives — sharp metrics, conversion lifts, or adoption stats from comparable startups give them the confidence to act quickly without belaboring the point.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. Present pricing and plan comparison directly after evidence so the Mover can evaluate options immediately. Clean, decisive framing — no friction between proof and purchase logic.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. For a Mover at decision stage, this is a strong, singular CTA — start a trial or choose a plan. No hesitation hooks, just a clear commitment prompt.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat as required. Provides secondary paths — contact sales, explore docs, or revisit pricing — catching any Mover who needs one more touchpoint before committing.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context: this is an AI-first, intent-aware design system built for startups. Movers need fast clarity on what this is and why it matters to them — no ambiguity, straight to the point.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. Movers respect proof but don't need lengthy narratives — sharp metrics, conversion lifts, or adoption stats from comparable startups give them the confidence to act quickly without belaboring the point.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Decision-stage requirement. Present pricing and plan comparison directly after evidence so the Mover can evaluate options immediately. Clean, decisive framing — no friction between proof and purchase logic.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. For a Mover at decision stage, this is a strong, singular CTA — start a trial or choose a plan. No hesitation hooks, just a clear commitment prompt.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion page requires 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' to eliminate friction and focus attention on the primary conversion action.

Decision-stage context with Mover archetype demands 'with-price for decision stage: centered offer presentation with price_display slot surfacing the price as a fact before the CTA' to anchor commitment and establish pricing transparency early.

slots.headline: Your design system finally knows why.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior to the right archetype, stage, and context — automatically.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Mover archetype suppresses secondary information; 'Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact' with '3-stat is the preferred default' delivers high-impact proof without distraction.

stats.label: From install to operational intelligence layer
Resolution dimensions: archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
Fully specified components with zone affinity and slot schemas

page.reasoning: Mover archetype in decision stage requires decisive framing; 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' with 'Mover variant strips description; price and CTA only' focuses conversion intent on the primary offer.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Yours forever.
features: Three palette presets out of the box
Next.js preview UI with palette overlays
Lifetime access, no subscription, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Mover archetype demands 'strong headline, single CTA, no subhead' for maximum conversion clarity and directness at the decision moment.

slots.headline: Ship smarter design. Once.
slots.subhead: One purchase. Full intelligence layer. Running in under an hour.
slots.trust_signal: No subscription. No runtime dependency. You own it.

page.reasoning: Conversion-stage pricing/signup context requires 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows' to minimize drop-off at page close.
